reason:
- hook_post_commit: run incremental_index() in the post-commit detached
  thread before hotspot trend recording, best-effort/non-fatal, 30s budget
- vector_store: lower ef_construction 200->100 to halve HNSW rebuild time
  with negligible recall impact (ef_search=100 compensates)
- scip/orchestrator: raise SCIP timeout 300s->600s for cold-cache rust-analyzer
  runs on large workspaces with vendored crates
summary: perf: auto-incremental-index on post-commit + lower HNSW build cost + raise SCIP timeout

reason:
rust-analyzer scip emits thousands of stderr warning lines. The
original code piped stderr but never read it until after wait_timeout,
causing the OS pipe buffer (~64KB on Windows) to fill and the child
to block on write() — a false timeout no matter how high the limit.

Fix: spawn background threads to drain stdout/stderr continuously while
the main thread waits for exit. Capture the last 8KB of stderr for
error reporting.
summary: fix: drain SCIP process pipes to prevent deadlock

reason: Adding toolchain: stable to 14 rust-toolchain actions that were missing the required input, causing cargo audit/deny to fail on every PR
summary: Fix missing toolchain inputs in CI workflows + review findings

reason:
All 14 dtolnay/rust-toolchain invocations across ci.yml, security.yml,
release.yml, and smoke.yml were missing the required 'toolchain' input,
causing cargo audit and cargo deny to fail with 'toolchain is a required
input' on every PR and push.
summary: fix(ci): add missing toolchain: stable to all rust-toolchain actions

reason: Defensive hardening of MCP tool results and LLM analysis pipeline against adversarial repo content; adds deterministic bounds and explicit cloud egress opt-in for MCP ask tool.
summary: Harden AI/agent boundary (0031): MCP sanitization, prompt injection mitigation, parse bounds, cloud egress gate

reason: Track 0031 implementation complete; clearing ledger before cross-model gate
summary: 0031: harden AI/agent boundary — MCP Markdown/HTML sanitization (split content vs structured), prompt injection mitigation (fence escaping, DATA framing), LLM parse bounds + fail-closed, cloud egress confused-deputy gate (mcp_allow_cloud_egress)

reason:
MCP tool-result hardening (DoD-1):
- New sanitize_mcp_content() escapes Markdown fences/images/links/headings/tables/HTML/control/bidi chars
- New sanitize_mcp_structured() preserves JSON structure while escaping Markdown in string values
- text_response uses full sanitize; json_response uses structured sanitize
- Subprocess stdout/stderr capped at 4MB to prevent OOM
- Adversarial tests: fence-breakout, image-exfil, zero-width bypass, nested fences, unicode, oversized

Pipeline integrity (DoD-2):
- escape_code_chunk replaces backticks with U+02CB before fencing (prevents fence-breakout)
- intent_drafter wraps git diff/commit as [Untrusted repository content] DATA with backtick escaping
- Unknown LLM categories/relations collected into parse_warnings Vec (not just warn!)
- parse_warnings surfaced in graph.rs

Parse robustness (DoD-3):
- 10MB response size limit, 10k nodes/edges cap, 10k field length cap
- Fail-closed: parse errors return Err with annotation (not silent skip)
- floor_char_boundary for safe UTF-8 truncation

Cloud egress gate (DoD-4):
- MCP ask tool forces --backend local by default (confused-deputy mitigation)
- New config.ask.mcp_allow_cloud_egress (default false) opts into cloud via MCP
- Trust boundary documented in review.md
- AI-path claims reconciled (no site drift found)
summary: fix(0031): harden AI/agent boundary — MCP sanitization, prompt injection mitigation, parse bounds, cloud egress gate

reason: Honest-zero #3: dashboard fetchTrends() was a mock returning empty array. Now the backend serves real project-level trend data from a precomputed rollup.
summary: Add GET /api/trends endpoint with cached daily rollup (migration m49, project_trend_days table). Rollup populated incrementally by post-commit hook + bounded 90-day catch-up. No per-request git walk.

reason:
- Migration m49: project_trend_days table (day TEXT PK, score, changes,
  high_risk_count) — m44-style, idempotent, unconditional
- Post-commit hook: upsert_project_trend_day aggregates the latest
  hotspot_trends snapshot for the UTC day into the rollup (Rust-side
  normalize_score log-scale transform, clamped 0-100)
- catch_up_project_trends: bounded 90-day idempotent backfill from
  existing hotspot_trends data (INSERT OR REPLACE on day PK)
- GET /api/trends endpoint (web-gated): returns TrendsResponse with
  TrendPointDto (camelCase wire matching frontend TrendPoint)
- Tests: 8 unit + 3 integration (empty/populated/off-by-one)
- coordination.md: §3.2 updated, §4.10 added, §11 changelog
- Codex cross-model review: 6 rounds, final PASS with 0 findings above LOW

reason:
- Migration m51: prev_hash column on ledger_entries + chain_head singleton table
- Chain hash: SHA-256(tx_id || signature_hex || prev_hash) per entry
- Signed chain head: binds (latest_entry_hash, genesis, length)
- CAS: conditional UPDATE with row-count check on chain_head
- Canonical ordering: committed_at ASC, tx_id ASC in all entry queries
- verify --signatures --chain: full-linkage walk, break localization
- Fail-closed on downgrade: stripped head + entries with prev_hash
- verify --against-export: rollback/tail-truncation detection
- Orphan head detection: empty entries + remaining chain_head
- Pre-chain ledgers: benign (no prev_hash, no head)
- SOC2 export: chain_head.json + prev_hash in ledger.csv
- Mode disclosure: real chain continuity status
- Re-sign: new chain segment + MAINTENANCE entry linking old->new head
- signing_required threaded into chain head signing (graceful degradation)
- 7 codex review rounds (final: CLEAN, no findings above LOW)
- 1459 unit + 560 integration tests pass

reason:
New 'export' cargo feature (in default + web) makes the SOC2 export
callable without the web feature. 'ledgerful export evidence --profile soc2'
with --out/--force uses 0032 path-safety (canonicalize, symlink re-check,
refuse src/state/Cargo.toml, refuse overwrite without --force).

Tests: 8 integration (parity, CLI/direct/web comparison, path-safety,
overwrite refusal, symlink re-check on Windows + Unix).
summary: feat(0039): export evidence --profile soc2 CLI command

reason:
First-class 'ledgerful demo' command: creates a synthetic invoice-service
repo, runs the real hook flow through 5 commit cycles producing real
Ed25519-signed entries from an ephemeral keypair, runs verify, and exports
a self-identifying DEMO SOC2 evidence zip.

- Ephemeral keys via HOME/USERPROFILE redirect to demo dir (0015 DI)
- Repo-local git config (user.name/email + core.hooksPath) for isolation
- DEMO marker: manifest demo:true, index.md, filename, entry summaries, stdout
- generate_soc2_export_with_options(layout, demo) for demo vs real exports
- 8 integration tests: hook-fired, keys-sentinel, manifest-demo, keep/cleanup,
  force, git-config-unchanged, stdout-markers
